zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Rossell於大約1550年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特時，他的父親John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar24歲，母親Annora Pierrepont15歲。他於1568年8月28日與Margaret Casse在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾可洛普維爾 畢曉普結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1630年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾克倫威爾享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Rossell于大约1550年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特时，他的父亲John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar24岁，母亲Annora Pierrepont15岁。他于1568年8月28日与Margaret Casse在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾可洛普維爾 畢曉普结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1630年在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾克倫威爾享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rossell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rosell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Teobaldo, Jorge, Pedro, Santiago, Ana, Carlos, Ernesto, Jose, Miguel, Modesto, Alberto.  Scandinavian Erik, Nils.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element ros ‘rose’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .

variants.name: Rozzell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Rossell   or   Russell  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of   Rossel   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Rozzelle  .

variants.name: Rosselle
meanings: Altered form of English   Rossell  .
French: from the feminine form of   Rossel   and a variant of   Rousselle  . Compare   Rozelle  .

variants.name: Rosel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Felicitas, Araceli, Armando, Arturo, Cristina, Esequiel, Francisco, Margarita, Nestor, Paciano, Roberto, Rodelio.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .
German (Rösel): from a pet form of Rose 1.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Rossel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Juan, Alvaro, Ana, Carols, Catarino, Guillermo, Javier, Jose, Orlando, Pedro, Ruben.  French Philippe, Yves.  Dutch Gerrit, Flor.
French, Dutch, and Flemish: nickname for a red-haired man, from a diminutive of Old French and Dutch ros ‘red’.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Catalan   Rossell  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Rossell was born about 1550, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar, was 24 and his mother, Annora Pierrepont, was 15. He married Margaret Casse on 28 August 1568, in Cropwell Bishop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1630, in Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Rossell est né environ 1550, à Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Annora Pierrepont, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Casse le 28 août 1568, à Cropwell Bishop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1630, à Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans.

Er hat am 28. August 1568 in Cropwell Bishop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Margaret Casse geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1630 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Rossellが約1550年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアホールメ ピエレプオントで生まれた時，父John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soarは24歳，母Annora Pierrepontは15歳であった。1568年8月28日にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクロプウエル ビショップでMargaret Casseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1630年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクロムウエルで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Rossell(이)가 대략 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Annora Pierrepont(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1568년 8월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 크럽웰 비숍에서 Margaret Casse(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1630년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 크롬웰에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Rossell родился около 1550 г. в местности холм пьиррепонт, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Annora Pierrepont, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Casse 28 августа 1568 г. в местности кропвелл бишоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1630 г. в местности кромвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет.
